title: Specify target adapter manifest
description: Refines `adapter.schema.json` for target adapters per workflow §Target adapter contract. Locks `axis` to the literal `target` and requires `briefs.shape`, `briefs.build`, and `briefs.merge` — together these close the operation set to `{shape, build, merge}` (the canonical iterator is `briefs.keys()`). The `kebabName` and `toolDeclaration` `$defs` are intentionally inlined (rather than `$ref`-resolved against `adapter.schema.json`) so the schema compiles without a registry lookup; keep the embedded `name` pattern byte-identical to `adapter.schema.json#/$defs/kebabName.pattern`.

inputs.description: Optional adapter-declared build inputs. Each entry is a path (relative to the build request's `inputs.root`, i.e. the slice tree) that the `build` operation consumes, flagged `required`. The CLI assembles `inputs.artifacts.additional[]` from this list and raises `target-build-input-missing` when a `required` path is absent. v1 keeps the declaration a flat path list; globs / conditional inputs are deferred.

platform.description: Closed target platform enum. `core` is the shared Rust business-logic crate; `ios`, `android` are supported shell platforms; `web`, `desktop` are future placeholders.

platformsCapability.description: Declarative platforms capability for target adapters. When `required` is true, `specrun init` demands `--platforms` and validation rejects a project that omits the set.

required.description: Whether projects using this target must declare a non-empty platform set.

allowed.description: The set of platforms this target accepts.

default.description: Default platform set used by greenfield scaffolding when the operator does not specify.
